name: Support / Resistance
description: Level-reaction model that trades clean break, reclaim, or rejection behavior around well-defined support and resistance zones.

context: Mark daily and weekly highs, lows, prior session levels, and untouched reaction zones.
Prefer levels with liquidity or repeated reactions, not random midrange points.
Know whether you want a breakout continuation or rejection before price touches the level.
setup: Price approaches a defined support or resistance with clean swings.
A sweep or rejection occurs at the level.
Structure around the level compresses enough to define invalidation.
trigger: Enter on reclaim of support or failure below resistance for rejection trades.
Enter on close and hold beyond the level for breakout trades.
Execute only when the level reaction is obvious and invalidation is defined.
confirmation: Multiple touches or volume make the level meaningful.
Wick rejection or closing behavior confirms the response at the level.
The next path of price is open enough for the trade to develop.
invalidation: Price accepts through the level and holds.
The level breaks with range expansion against the thesis.
Reaction stays noisy and whipsaws both sides of the zone.
noTrade: The level is already overused and reactions are decaying.
Another stronger level sits too close behind entry.
The approach is messy with no clean boundary.

stopLoss: Place stop beyond the far side of the zone or sweep extreme.
Use the full zone width to judge whether the trade is valid.
If the zone is too wide, skip the setup.
takeProfit: Target the next clear level or liquidity pool.
Pay partials quickly on countertrend reactions.
Hold the rest only if breakout acceptance or clean rejection continuation develops.
timeExit: Close if price cannot leave the zone quickly.
Flatten before session close if the trade depends on active liquidity.
Do not carry a level reaction after the level has already been consumed.
earlyExit: The zone is retaken with conviction.
Repeated tests erode the reaction edge after entry.
